title: I built a full SaaS in 48 hours with AI. Here's what I learned
post:
48 hours. That's how long it took me to go from idea to a functioning SaaS prototype with paying users. No co-founder. No developer. Just me, Claude, Cursor, and a lot of coffee.

What I learned: AI doesn't eliminate the need for product thinking — it amplifies it. I still had to know what to build, who to build it for, and how to position it. But instead of spending 6 months figuring out how to code my vision, I spent 48 hours executing it. The gap between thinking and building has collapsed.

The scary part? This is just the beginning. Tools are getting better every week. The question isn't whether you can build something fast — it's whether you have the ideas worth building.

What's stopping you from turning your idea into a product right now? Thoughts?
